2603.11304.title: Worst-case low-rank approximations
2603.11304.paper_summary: Real-world data in health, economics, and environmental sciences are often collected across heterogeneous domains (such as hospitals, regions, or time periods). In such settings, distributional shifts can make standard PCA unreliable, in that, for example, the leading principal components may explain substantially less variance in unseen domains than in the training domains. Existing approaches (such as FairPCA) have proposed to consider worst-case (rather than average) performance across multiple domains. This work develops a unified framework, called wcPCA, applies it to other objectives (resulting in the novel estimators such as norm-minPCA and norm-maxregret, which are better suited for applications with heterogeneous total variance) and analyzes their relationship. We prove that for all objectives, the estimators are worst-case optimal not only over the observed source domains but also over all target domains whose covariance lies in the convex hull of the (possibly normalized) source covariances. We establish consistency and asymptotic worst-case guarantees of empirical estimators. We extend our methodology to matrix completion, another problem that makes use of low-rank approximations, and prove approximate worst-case optimality for inductive matrix completion. Simulations and two real-world applications on ecosystem-atmosphere fluxes demonstrate marked improvements in worst-case performance, with only minor losses in average performance.
2603.11304.paper_summary_zh: 現實世界中的健康、經濟和環境科學數據通常是在異質領域（例如醫院、地區或時間段）中收集的。在這種情況下，分佈變化可能使標準主成分分析（PCA）變得不可靠，例如，主要的主成分在未見過的領域中解釋的變異量可能遠低於訓練領域。現有的方法（如FairPCA）已提出考慮多個領域中的最壞情況（而不是平均）表現。本研究開發了一個統一框架，稱為wcPCA，並將其應用於其他目標（產生了如norm-minPCA和norm-maxregret等新估計量，更適合於具有異質總變異的應用），並分析它們之間的關係。我們證明對於所有目標，這些估計量在觀察到的源領域以及所有其協方差位於（可能經過標準化的）源協方差的凸包中的目標領域上都是最壞情況下的最優。 我們建立了經驗估計量的一致性和漸近最壞情況保證。我們將我們的方法擴展到矩陣補全，這是另一個利用低秩近似的問題，並證明了歸納矩陣補全的近似最壞情況最優性。模擬和兩個關於生態系統-大氣通量的實際應用顯示在最壞情況下的表現有顯著改善，平均表現僅有輕微損失。
2603.11304.author: Anya Fries et.al.
2603.11304.authors: Anya Fries, Markus Reichstein, David Blei, Jonas Peters
